summary: Iteration 21: Complete E2E testing of Data-Driven Mapping UI. All 16 backend tests pass, all frontend tests pass. The new system replaces manual keyword entry with checkbox-based selection from uploaded Naukri job titles.

backend: POST /api/upload/naukri - extracts distinct job titles into job_titles_master collection
POST /api/job-keyword-mappings - creates mapping and sets is_mapped=true for keywords
After creating mapping, GET /api/job-titles/unmatched does NOT include the mapped keywords
DELETE /api/job-keyword-mappings - releases keywords back to unmatched (is_mapped=false)
PUT /api/job-keyword-mappings - handles keyword add/remove correctly (maps new, unmaps removed)
Job role normalization uses exact match (not substring) on normalized titles
GET /api/job-roles uses normalized roles from mappings
POST /api/job-keyword-mappings requires at least one keyword (400 for empty)
DELETE /api/job-keyword-mappings returns 400 for invalid ID format
Full CRUD cycle for mappings (create, read, update, delete)
frontend: /jobs-keywords page loads with 'Mapped Roles' and 'Unmatched Keywords' sections
'Unmatched Keywords' section shows amber keyword pills after Naukri data upload
'Add Mapping' button opens modal with role input and checkbox list
Modal checkbox list shows unmatched keywords
Modal has filter/search input for keywords
Saving creates mapping and shows 'Mapping created' toast
After save, keywords move from unmatched to mapped section
Edit button opens modal with 'Edit Mapping' title
Edit modal shows pre-filled role name
Edit modal shows pre-checked keywords (6 SELECTED)
Edit modal shows 'Update' button instead of 'Save'
After delete, keywords return to unmatched section
No manual keyword typing input (+ button removed) - checkbox-only selection

critical_code_review_comments: is_mapped flag correctly tracks keyword mapping status in job_titles_master collection
_sync_job_titles_master() properly extracts distinct job titles from naukri_applies
Exact match normalization prevents substring matching issues
PUT endpoint correctly handles keyword add/remove with is_mapped flag updates
DELETE endpoint releases keywords only if not used by another mapping
seed_data_creation: Test data created via Naukri CSV upload with TEST_ prefixed job titles. Test mappings created and deleted during testing (TEST_ prefixed mappings cleaned up automatically).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Data-Driven Mapping UI fully tested and working. Key changes from previous iteration: (1) Job titles now come from uploaded Naukri data into job_titles_master collection, (2) GET /api/job-titles/unmatched returns titles with is_mapped=false, (3) Mapping done via checkbox selection in modal (not manual typing), (4) is_mapped flag tracks whether a keyword is mapped, (5) Keywords map to only ONE canonical role, (6) Exact match on normalized values for job role resolution. The frontend /jobs-keywords page has data-testid attributes: jobs-keywords-page, add-mapping-btn, mapping-modal, role-input, keyword-checkbox-list, keyword-option-{i}, keyword-checkbox-{i}, save-mapping-btn, unmatched-keywords-list, unmatched-keyword-{i}, edit-mapping-{id}, delete-mapping-{id}.
